sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Tractor Supply (NASDAQ:TSCO) Earns Buy Rating from DA Davidson
feed.summary: DA Davidson has reiterated a "buy" rating for Tractor Supply (NASDAQ:TSCO) with a $60 price target, aligning with the analyst consensus of a "Moderate Buy" and an average target of $60.05. This comes despite Tractor Supply missing Q4 earnings and revenue expectations and lowering its FY2026 guidance, which led to a share selloff and target reductions from other analysts. Institutional investors continue to show strong interest, with Vanguard Group Inc. and Wellington Management Group LLP increasing their stakes in the company.

feed.title: Cincinnati Financial increases quarterly dividend by 8%
feed.summary: Cincinnati Financial Corporation (NASDAQ:CINF) has announced an 8% increase in its quarterly cash dividend, raising it from 87 cents to 94 cents per share. This marks the 66th consecutive year of dividend increases for the company, making it one of the longest streaks among U.S. public companies. The dividend is payable on April 15, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 24, 2026.

feed.title: Campbell’s to shutter Cape Cod potato chip plant
feed.summary: Campbell’s is closing its Cape Cod potato chip plant in Hyannis, Massachusetts, by April, affecting 49 employees. The company stated the facility, which also produces Kettle Brand chips, "no longer makes economic sense" as it accounts for only 4% of the total annual Cape Cod chip volume. This move is part of Campbell's broader strategy to consolidate operations and boost efficiency across its snack business.

feed.title: Boston Scientific (BSX) Garnering Bullish Views Amid Penumbra Acquisition
feed.summary: Boston Scientific (NYSE: BSX) is receiving bullish views from analysts following its strategic acquisition of Penumbra (PEN). Truist Securities reiterated a Buy rating with a $120 price target, citing the deal's potential to boost durability and double-digit revenue/EPS growth. Morgan Stanley also maintains a positive outlook, highlighting "clear catalysts" that make the stock's valuation attractive.

feed.title: Cincinnati Financial lifts dividend 8% to $0.94 | CINF Stock News
feed.summary: Cincinnati Financial Corporation (CINF) has announced an 8% increase in its regular quarterly cash dividend, raising it from $0.87 to $0.94 per share. This dividend is payable on April 15, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 24, 2026, marking what is expected to be the company's 66th consecutive year of dividend increases. The company emphasized its financial strength and successful strategy, including its focus on independent agents, as drivers for continued shareholder value and future rewards.

feed.title: New actuarial, reinsurance and accounting leaders at Cincinnati Financial
feed.summary: Cincinnati Financial (CINF) announced key executive appointments following its subsidiaries' meetings on January 30, 2026. Luyang Fu was named senior vice president and chief actuary, R. Phillip Sandercox became senior vice president and head of Cincinnati Re, and Andrew M. Schnell was appointed senior vice president and treasurer. These appointments highlight the company's focus on strengthening leadership in actuarial, reinsurance, and accounting operations ahead of upcoming financial results.

feed.title: 3 charged after stealing more than $18,000 from Ulta Beauty stores, records show
feed.summary: Three individuals—Yilianne Casero, Yoandys Diaz, and Sariel Maqueira—have been charged with stealing over $18,000 worth of merchandise from Ulta Beauty stores in East Tennessee. The trio was apprehended after being spotted leaving an Ulta Beauty and subsequently stopped on Interstate 40, where stolen goods and drugs were discovered in their vehicle. They face charges including two counts of theft of merchandise and one count under the Organized Retail Crime Prevention Act.

feed.title: First Solar Shares Rebound As Wall Street Dismisses Tesla Competition Concerns
feed.summary: First Solar Inc. (FSLR) shares rebounded after Wall Street analysts dismissed concerns about Tesla's solar expansion plans significantly impacting First Solar's earnings through at least 2030 due to cost advantages and supply chain challenges. While Mizuho and Wells Fargo remain optimistic about First Solar, BMO Capital downgraded it to 'Market Perform' due to potential long-term overhang from Tesla's efforts to build a vertically integrated solar manufacturing base. Tesla CEO Elon Musk recently reiterated a target of 100 gigawatts of annual solar manufacturing within three years, causing FSLR shares to dip initially.

feed.title: Principal Financial Group CEO sells $884,616 in stock
feed.summary: Deanna D. Strable-Soethout, CEO of Principal Financial Group Inc, sold 9,300 shares of company stock for $884,616 on January 29, 2026. This transaction was conducted under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. JPMorgan recently downgraded Principal Financial Group to Neutral with a $103 price target, citing better value in other life insurance stocks.

feed.title: Costco stock price slips today as Instacart takes same‑day delivery to France and Spain
feed.summary: Costco's shares slipped by 1.3% after Instacart expanded their same-day delivery partnership to France and Spain. This move aims to increase customer engagement and order frequency by offering a convenient online shopping experience. Investors are watching closely to see if digital order growth can boost sales without negatively impacting margins, with upcoming sales figures and earnings reports anticipated.

feed.title: Southwest Airlines stock slips as LUV cools off after 18.7% surge on profit forecast
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines (LUV) stock declined by 1.1% to $47.99, cooling off after an 18.7% surge driven by an upgraded annual profit outlook and a new fee-based restructuring strategy. This shift includes new revenue streams like bag fees and assigned seating, moving away from its traditional no-frills model. BMO Capital upgraded the stock to "outperform" with a price target of $57.50, reflecting confidence in the airline's initiatives despite potential customer resistance to higher costs.

feed.title: Arista Networks's Options Frenzy: What You Need to Know
feed.summary: Deep-pocketed investors have shown a mixed sentiment towards Arista Networks (NYSE: ANET), with extraordinary options activity detected. While 39% of investors are bullish, 42% are bearish, and the projected price targets range from $120.0 to $210.0. ANET's stock is currently down, and an analyst recently upgraded its rating to Overweight with a $159 price target.

feed.title: Tandem Investment Advisors Inc. Has $37.86 Million Position in Essential Utilities Inc. $WTRG
feed.summary: Tandem Investment Advisors Inc. reduced its stake in Essential Utilities Inc. (NYSE:WTRG) by 54.2% in the last quarter, yet still holds 948,863 shares valued at $37.86 million. Despite this insider selling, institutional ownership remains strong at nearly 74.8%, with firms like Norges Bank and Vanguard Group Inc. increasing their positions. Essential Utilities recently announced a quarterly dividend, and analysts have a consensus "Hold" rating with an average target price of $43.29.

feed.title: Piper Sandler Maintains Overweight Rating on Stryker Corporation (SYK)
feed.summary: Piper Sandler has reiterated its Overweight rating on Stryker Corporation (SYK) with a price target of $420, citing a new handheld orthopedic robot, Mako RPS, which is expected to expand the company's presence in lower-cost markets. This move positions Stryker to compete more effectively in specific segments, as noted by analyst Matthew O’Brien. Earlier, Evercore ISI also maintained an Outperform rating, lowering its target slightly to $390, based on market recovery and healthy capital expenditure expectations in the medical technology sector for 2026.

feed.title: Citigroup Has Lowered Expectations for Norfolk Southern (NYSE:NSC) Stock Price
feed.summary: Citigroup has reduced its price target for Norfolk Southern (NYSE:NSC) from $317 to $300, maintaining a neutral rating despite the company's Q4 EPS beat ($3.22 vs. $2.76) and improved operating ratio. The lowered target reflects a revenue miss ($3.0B vs. $3.03B) and weaker volumes, with other analysts like Barclays and Royal Bank Of Canada also lowering their targets. The stock was trading around $290.71, with institutional ownership at approximately 75% and mixed recent insider activity.

feed.title: Entergy extends dividend streak since 1988 with $0.64 payout
feed.summary: Entergy (NYSE:ETR) has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.64 per share. This dividend is payable on March 2, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 9, 2026. The company has a long history of consistent dividend payments, having paid a cash dividend on its common stock continuously since 1988.

feed.title: $20M facelift planned at Cincinnati-area townhome community
feed.summary: Blackstone's affordable housing division, April Housing, is investing over $20 million to renovate a 240-unit townhome community in the Cincinnati area. This project at Tyler’s Creek Townhomes in Fairfield Township and the city of Hamilton marks the company's third such endeavor in the region since 2022. The renovation aims to give the community a significant facelift.

feed.title: Q4 Preliminary Figures and 2026 Guidance Make DexCom (DXCM) Look Attractive
feed.summary: DexCom (DXCM) has been rated attractive due to strong preliminary Q4 results and positive 2026 guidance. The medical device company, known for its continuous glucose monitoring systems, beat consensus estimates for Q4 revenues and provided optimistic projections for gross and operating margins in 2025 and 2026. Analysts currently assign a median 1-year price target implying a 17% upside.

feed.title: TD Cowen Raises Price Target for Waste Management (WM) to $270
feed.summary: TD Cowen has increased its price target for Waste Management (WM) from $265 to $270, while maintaining a "Buy" rating. This decision reflects continued confidence in the stock's outlook, despite varying price target adjustments from other analysts this month. Waste Management remains the largest integrated provider of traditional solid waste services in the United States.

feed.title: JP Morgan Lowers Price Target for Norfolk Southern (NSC) | NSC S
feed.summary: JP Morgan has maintained a "Neutral" rating for Norfolk Southern (NSC) but has lowered its price target from $301 to $296, a decrease of 1.66%. The average target price from 18 analysts is $307.35, suggesting a 6.29% upside, while GuruFocus's GF Value estimates a 12.65% downside from the current price. The consensus brokerage recommendation for NSC is currently "Hold."

feed.title: Shareholders Overwhelmingly Approve Kimberly-Clark’s Acquisition of Kenvue
feed.summary: Shareholders of Kimberly-Clark and Kenvue have overwhelmingly approved the acquisition of Kenvue by Kimberly-Clark for $48.7 billion. This merger is expected to create a global health and wellness leader with combined annual net revenues of approximately $32 billion and adjusted EBITDA of about $7 billion, aiming to accelerate innovation and expand brand access. The transaction is projected to close in the second half of 2026, pending regulatory approvals.

feed.title: Hoka and Ugg’s Stellar Q3 Sends Deckers Stock Soaring, Quiets Wall Street Naysayers
feed.summary: Deckers Outdoor Corp. (DECK), the parent company of Ugg and Hoka brands, reported stellar Q3 earnings, sending its stock soaring and alleviating Wall Street's concerns about a sales slowdown. Both brands showed significant global demand and growth in both direct-to-consumer and wholesale channels, exceeding expectations. Despite the positive results, analysts maintained "Buy" or "Hold" ratings, noting the need for sustained momentum and the early stages of Hoka's expansion into lifestyle markets.

feed.title: Tesla bulls and bears agree: This is not an auto stock
feed.summary: Tesla's recent plans to halt production of its higher-end Model S and X vehicles and focus on autonomous driving, robotics, and energy storage have led analysts, both bullish and bearish, to agree that Tesla is no longer just an auto stock. This shift is seen as a move towards "Physical AI" and robotics, despite automotive sales still contributing significantly to revenue and the company's valuation being much higher than traditional carmakers. Analysts express caution about the stock's high valuation and the projected increase in capital expenditures without a clear commitment to delivery growth.

feed.title: AVAV or LHX: Which Defense Stock Looks Stronger Right Now?
feed.summary: This article compares two defense stocks, AeroVironment (AVAV) and L3Harris Technologies (LHX), highlighting their strengths amid increasing global defense spending. While AVAV focuses on unmanned systems and advanced airspace, LHX is a diversified contractor with strong financial performance and international programs. The analysis concludes that LHX offers a more compelling investment opportunity due to its diversified business, steady financials, and attractive valuation.

feed.title: Breaking Down ResMed: 7 Analysts Share Their Views
feed.summary: Seven analysts have provided diverse ratings for ResMed (NYSE: RMD) over the last three months, with most holding an "Indifferent" stance. The average 12-month price target is $279.86, a slight decrease from the previous average. ResMed demonstrates strong financial performance with an above-average market cap, 9.07% revenue growth, and high net margin, ROE, and ROA, alongside sound debt management.

feed.title: Kroger opens $14M store with fuel station in NKY: PHOTOS
feed.summary: Kroger has opened a new $14 million store in Northern Kentucky, featuring a fuel station, a Murray's Cheese Shop, and a sushi kiosk, with plans for further additions later this year. The article, by Andy Brownfield of the Cincinnati Business Courier, provides a preview of the new location.

feed.title: Short Interest in Surf Air Mobility Inc. (NYSE:SRFM) Declines By 42.0%
feed.summary: Short interest in Surf Air Mobility Inc. (NYSE:SRFM) significantly decreased by 42.0% to 3,980,942 shares by January 15th, indicating a lower bearish sentiment. Despite this, analysts maintain a cautious "Hold" rating with an average target price of $6.50, and the company recently missed its quarterly EPS estimates. Institutional investors have shown varied activity, with some increasing their stakes in the company.

feed.title: Lamb Weston Enhances Shareholder Returns Amid Operational Restructuring
feed.summary: Lamb Weston is focusing on both increasing shareholder returns and streamlining its operations. The company announced a 3% increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.38 per share and has repurchased $39.6 million in stock during Q2 fiscal 2026. Concurrently, Lamb Weston is consolidating its Latin American production by closing its Munro facility and shifting operations to a more modern plant in Mar del Plata, aiming for improved efficiency.

feed.title: Nvidia's new HQ building to house thousands of workers
feed.summary: Nvidia has unveiled plans for a new headquarters building on its Santa Clara campus, designed to accommodate 3,000 employees. The company shared construction timelines during a recent community meeting, detailing the groundbreaking and project completion schedules. This expansion highlights Nvidia's continued growth and investment in its physical infrastructure.

feed.title: Newmont stock price slides as gold plunges after Trump taps Kevin Warsh for Fed
feed.summary: Newmont's stock price dropped significantly by 12% to $111.64 after spot gold plunged 8.9% following Donald Trump's announcement to nominate Kevin Warsh as head of the Federal Reserve. This sharp decline in gold prices, which also impacted other precious metals and mining stocks, is attributed to profit-taking by investors and a strengthening dollar. Investors are now closely watching Newmont's upcoming Q4 and full-year 2025 results on February 19 for future insights.

feed.title: M&T Bank Corp stock hits 52-week high at $222.21
feed.summary: M&T Bank Corp (MTB) has reached a new 52-week high at $222.21, reflecting a 10.32% increase over the past year due to strong financial results and positive investor sentiment. The bank also reported strong Q4 earnings of $4.72 per share, exceeding analyst expectations, and RBC Capital raised its price target to $220, citing confidence in the bank's "meat and potatoes" business model.

feed.title: National Health Investors stock hits 52-week high at $81.56 By Investing.com
feed.summary: National Health Investors Inc. (NHI) stock has reached a new 52-week high of $81.56, reflecting a 24.26% total return over the past year. The company, a real estate investment trust specializing in senior housing and medical properties, boasts a market capitalization of $3.88 billion and has maintained dividend payments for 35 consecutive years, offering a 4.56% dividend yield. This performance follows strategic investments, including a recent $89.2 million in healthcare investments and an upward adjustment in its price target by Truist Securities.

feed.title: Marco Technologies Named HP Inc. 2025 U.S. Commercial Supplies Partner of the Year
feed.summary: HP Inc. has recognized Marco Technologies as its 2025 U.S. Commercial Supplies Partner of the Year, praising Marco's exceptional performance, growth, and impact within the HP ecosystem. Marco was chosen for its significant achievements in growth, execution, and partnership excellence, demonstrating its commitment to delivering value and innovative solutions to customers. This award highlights Marco's successful collaboration with HP in providing scalable, secure, and flexible print programs for organizations with complex needs.

feed.title: Unsure if Kevin Warsh will be as dovish as Trump wants in 2027: Morgan Stanley's Andrew Slimmon
feed.summary: Andrew Slimmon, Senior Portfolio Manager at Morgan Stanley, discusses the potential nomination of Kevin Warsh to head the Federal Reserve under a Trump administration. The article provides video clips of various experts debating Warsh's suitability for the role, with opinions ranging from him being a "fantastic pick" to a "sock puppet" for Trump. The core concern revolves around whether Warsh would maintain a dovish stance, aligning with Trump's perceived preferences for the Fed going into 2027.

feed.title: Fastenal Stock Is Up 20% Over the Past Year. Here’s How Much Upside It Could Have in 2026
feed.summary: Fastenal Company (FAST) stock has risen 20% over the past year and analysts predict further upside. A valuation model suggests the stock could reach $57.53 by December 2028, representing a 32.8% total upside or a 10.2% annualized return over 2.9 years, based on assumptions of 8.9% revenue growth and 20.8% operating margins. The company, which distributes industrial and construction supplies, reports $8 billion in LTM revenue with 21% operating margins and a market capitalization of $25 billion.

feed.title: American Express, credit card provider to the wealthy, wants even more high spenders
feed.summary: American Express is shifting its marketing strategy to focus on high-spending customers, particularly its refreshed Platinum card with an $895 annual fee. This move reflects the "K-shaped" economic recovery, where affluent consumers continue to spend freely. The company reported fourth-quarter earnings slightly below expectations due to increased costs associated with the Platinum card refresh.

feed.title: Assessing Allison Transmission (ALSN) Valuation After Recent Share Price Momentum And Conflicting Fair Value Signals
feed.summary: Allison Transmission (ALSN) has shown strong recent share price momentum, with a 12.8% return in the last month and 35.1% over three months. Despite this, there are conflicting signals regarding its valuation; a popular narrative suggests it's 11.3% overvalued at $111.56 with a fair value of $100.20, while a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model indicates it might be 56.5% undervalued with an estimated future cash flow value of $256.55. The narrative's overvaluation assessment is influenced by projected growth in electrified powertrain segments and specific earnings multiples, while the DCF model points to a much higher intrinsic value based on future cash flows.

feed.title: Grocery stores restock after winter storm depletes shelves
feed.summary: Following a severe winter storm, grocery stores in Kentucky, including Kroger, Walmart, and Meijer, are actively replenishing their shelves after a surge in customer purchases left many aisles bare. Kroger, which faced distribution challenges due to the storm impacting their Nashville site, confirmed that their stores are now largely back to normal operations with nonperishable items restocked. Companies are utilizing established protocols to prepare for and recover from such weather-related disruptions.

feed.title: Power-hungry grid: NRG doubles fleet with 13 GW plants and VPP buy
feed.summary: NRG Energy has completed its acquisition of 13 GW of generation assets and CPower from LS Power, effectively doubling its generation capacity to approximately 25 GW. This strategic move, which includes 18 natural-gas-fired facilities and a commercial and industrial virtual power plant (C&I VPP) platform, aims to enhance grid reliability and affordability for NRG's eight million customers amidst growing energy demand. The company emphasizes its commitment to providing flexible and resilient energy solutions for various customer types, from data centers to households.

feed.title: Coca-Cola stock hits all-time high at 74.38 USD
feed.summary: Coca-Cola (KO) stock has reached an all-time high of $74.38, driven by an 18.03% increase over the past year, strong investor confidence, and favorable market conditions. The company's stability is highlighted by its low beta of 0.39 and a 2.78% dividend yield, along with 55 consecutive years of dividend raises. Recent leadership changes and strategic considerations like increased demand for protein sports drinks and potential acquisitions by other companies in the coffee market also reflect the dynamic beverage industry landscape.

feed.title: What Price Targets Have Wall Street Analysts Set for Ingersoll Rand Shares?
feed.summary: Ingersoll Rand's stock performance has rebounded in 2026, surpassing the S&P 500, despite lagging in the previous year. Analysts have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for IR, with an average price target indicating a modest upside. The company's recent acquisition of Scinomix and past earnings have influenced stock fluctuations.

feed.title: The Goldman Sachs Group Forecasts Strong Price Appreciation for Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) Stock
feed.summary: The Goldman Sachs Group raised its price target for Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) to $32 from $29 but maintained a "sell" rating, suggesting a significant downside. Despite a Q4 EPS beat and strong FY 2026 guidance influenced by new strategies like assigned seating, weak Q1 guidance and mixed analyst ratings create near-term risk. Overall, analysts have a "Hold" consensus on the stock with a target price of $44.76.

feed.title: Appeals court revives lawsuit from ex-managers who refused Hasbro's vaccine requirement
feed.summary: An appeals court has revived a lawsuit brought by two former Hasbro managers who claim their religious rights were violated when they were terminated for refusing the company's COVID-19 vaccine mandate. This decision allows their case against Hasbro Inc. to proceed after earlier dismissal.

feed.title: Royal Bank Of Canada Forecasts Strong Price Appreciation for Trane Technologies (NYSE:TT) Stock
feed.summary: Royal Bank of Canada raised its price target for Trane Technologies (NYSE:TT) to $470, indicating a potential 12.54% upside. This follows Trane Technologies beating Q4 earnings and revenue estimates and subsequently raising its FY2026 revenue guidance. The stock currently holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average target price of $482.13 from various analysts, despite a slight dip in Friday's trading.

feed.title: Royal Bank Of Canada Reaffirms "Outperform" Rating for Visa (NYSE:V)
feed.summary: Royal Bank of Canada has reaffirmed its "Outperform" rating for Visa (NYSE:V) with a $395 price target, indicating a potential 22.01% upside. The reaffirmation follows Visa's strong Q1 results, where it surpassed revenue and EPS expectations due to robust holiday payment volumes and highlighted strategic growth into stablecoin settlement and crypto rails. Despite insider share sales totaling approximately $8.25 million, institutional ownership of the company remains high at around 82.15%.

feed.title: Target prepares to open first Oak Cliff store
feed.summary: Target is preparing to open its first store in Oak Cliff, Dallas, an area that has previously lacked grocery options and significant retail investment. This opening is part of Target's strategy to open approximately 30 new stores nationwide each year through 2023. The article highlights the significance of this development for the Oak Cliff community.

feed.title: Simon Property Q4 earnings preview: What to expect (SPG:NYSE)
feed.summary: Simon Property (SPG) is set to release its Q4 earnings results on Monday, February 2nd, after the market closes. Analysts anticipate a consensus FFO estimate of $3.49 and a revenue estimate of $1.51 billion, marking a 5.34% year-over-year increase. The upcoming announcement follows an upward revision of its full-year guidance in the previous quarter.

feed.title: Parker Hannifin (PH) Receives Raised Price Target from JP Morgan
feed.summary: JP Morgan analyst Stephen Tusa has raised the price target for Parker Hannifin (PH) to $1100, maintaining an 'Overweight' rating, reflecting continued confidence in the company's performance. This adjustment follows several other analysts who have also recently increased their price targets for PH. Despite an average target price from Wall Street analysts suggesting a 5.71% upside, GuruFocus estimates a 35.06% downside based on its GF Value fair value calculation.

feed.title: Otis Worldwide (OTIS) Downgraded by JPMorgan to Neutral, Price Target Reduced
feed.summary: JPMorgan downgraded Otis Worldwide (OTIS) from Overweight to Neutral, reducing its price target to $98 from $116 due to underwhelming Q4 sales and guidance in the New Equipment sector. Despite this, Otis, the largest global elevator and escalator supplier with an 18% market share, maintains robust financial health with strong profitability margins. Investors are advised to consider these factors alongside sector-specific risks and overall market sentiment when evaluating the company's future prospects.

feed.title: Citi Lowers PT on PDD Holdings to $142, Keeps Neutral Rating Ahead of Q4 2025 Earnings
feed.summary: Citi has lowered its price target on PDD Holdings (NASDAQ: PDD) to $142 from $170, while maintaining a Neutral rating, citing expectations of slowing domestic retail sales and higher expenses affecting profitability in 2026. Despite the downgrade, the new price target still implies over 35% upside. The move comes ahead of the company's Q4 2025 earnings release, with previous reporting highlighting a "smaller-than-feared" fine from the State Taxation Administration of Shanghai as a positive development.
